preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced velar plosive /g/ to a voiceless dental fricative /θ/ is a complex shift involving changes in voicing, manner, and place of articulation. This shift is primarily attested as a conditioned sound change, often occurring due to assimilation to a following consonant.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (plosive to fricative), devoicing, and place assimilation (velar to dental)

examples.note: In specific contexts, such as before certain consonants, the underlying /g/ can be realized as [θ]. Examples include 'dignidad' (dignity) pronounced [diθ.ni.ˈðaθ] and 'obtener' (to obtain) pronounced [oθ.te.ˈneɾ].

sources: Colina, S., & Martínez-Gil, F. (Eds.). (2015). The Routledge Handbook of Spanish Phonology. Routledge.
